`ddx cleanup --apply` can delete stale DDx execution files, but it cannot terminate stale DDx-owned attempt-descendant process groups that are provably orphaned. Heavy leftover processes can continue burning CPU or holding ports after their original attempt is gone.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` applies file cleanup for stale temp dirs, worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, logs, worker dirs, and refs, but contains no safe process-group termination path.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` has no process reaping counts or warnings in `ExecutionCleanupSummary`, so apply mode cannot report terminated, preserved, blocked, or unsafe process groups.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:82-100` and `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:155-168` intentionally gate startup harness reaping on top-level harness names and dead bead lease owners; that does not cover descendant process groups whose harness root has already exited.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` sets process group and parent-death attributes for normal harness children, which cleanup can use only when stale ownership and liveness evidence prove the group is DDx-owned and safe to reap.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend execution cleanup apply mode with an injectable process-group killer and conservative stale ownership checks. Apply may terminate only process groups associated with the current project's DDx execution root where run-state cleanup liveness is expired or missing, the recorded owner PID is gone, the worktree is under the current project execution root, and no live worker sidecar or active registered worktree owns it. Preserve live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, other-project processes, and any uncertain candidate. Prefer graceful terminate followed by bounded kill where feasible. Surface blocked warnings instead of silently deleting files when a process is detected but cannot be killed safely.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-e0de6369`; that bead owns `.ddx/harness-sessions/` scratch ignore/prune behavior.
- Do not duplicate `ddx-bbea954f`; that bead owns dirty timeout worktree edit preservation before cleanup deletion.
- Do not replace the existing parent-death/process-group fixes from `ddx-0d7d5f0e`, `ddx-3113d524`, or `ddx-418c3849`.
- Do not implement a general-purpose port scanner or host process manager. Port conflict recovery here comes only from safely reaping stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
- Do not kill processes outside the current project's DDx execution root, processes tied to live run-state/worker liveness, active registered worktrees, or non-DDx user workloads.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS
- `spec:FEAT-010`
- `spec:SD-025`

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker reliability and Niflheim local gates).
Deps: none.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyReapsOnlyStaleAttemptProcessGroups` using injected scanner/killer fakes where needed, proving `--apply` terminates only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups.
2. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyPreservesLiveAndForeignProcessGroups` proving live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, other-project processes, and uncertain candidates are preserved with operator-visible warnings when appropriate.
3. Add `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyTerminatesGracefullyThenBoundedKill` or an equivalent assertion proving the killer uses graceful terminate before bounded kill when a process group remains alive.
4. Existing process-reaper tests remain green: `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'ExecutionCleanupManager_Apply.*Process|ProcessReap|AttemptDescendant|OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:iterate, area:agent, area:cleanup, kind:bug, kind:reliability, operator-ux, resource-exhaustion, incident:niflheim-2026-06-08, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-2f8e5f26" base-rev="3d0e3a8616b6fca915fe00fb80d47e0792affaf4" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260628T044124-933dc737"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. DDx cleanup can delete stale execution worktrees and run-state files but still leave descendant shell, cargo, or test-server processes burning CPU or holding ports after the original attempt is gone. The operator sees clean files, but the host still carries DDx-owned work.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` only scans temp dirs, scratch roots, run-state files, evidence bundles, and worker dirs; it has no process census or process-group reaper.
`cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:42-152` only reaps PID-1 children whose argv basename matches a top-level harness (`claude`, `codex`, `gemini`, `opencode`, `pi`) and whose lease owner PID is gone, so it misses shell/cargo/test-server descendants under a stale DDx worktree.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a conservative DDx-owned attempt-process scanner/reaper seam under `cli/internal/agent/` that can inspect /proc-style facts (pid, ppid, pgid when available, command, cwd, start time, ownership/worktree context) and classify stale attempt descendant process groups.
- Extend `ExecutionCleanupSummary` with structured process findings and warnings so callers can report `would_kill` in dry-run and terminated groups in apply mode.
- Add Linux and unsupported-platform implementations plus injected fake scanner/killer tests. Use DDx ownership, cwd/worktree, liveness, and recorded run-state evidence to decide staleness.
- Reuse `/proc` parsing helpers already in `cli/internal/workerstatus/` where practical, but keep the cleanup-specific ownership rules in the agent package.

NON-SCOPE
- CLI JSON/text shaping in `cli/cmd/cleanup.go` is handled by a sibling bead.
- Startup/pre-claim wiring in `cli/cmd/startup_housekeeping.go` is handled by a sibling bead.
- Documentation updates in `docs/` are handled separately.
- No general-purpose host process manager, no command-name-only matching, and no killing outside the current project execution root.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecutionCleanupManager_DryRunReportsStaleAttemptDescendantProcesses` proves a stale process whose cwd/worktree is under the current project's DDx execution root is reported with pid, command, worktree, stale reason, and `would_kill=true`, while dry-run does not call the killer.
2. `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ApplyReapsOnlyStaleAttemptProcessGroups` proves `--apply` terminates only stale DDx-owned attempt process groups and preserves live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, and other-project processes.
3. `TestExecutionCleanupManager_ReportsNonHarnessDescendantByOwnership` (or equivalent) covers a non-harness descendant command such as `sh`, `cargo`, or a tiny long-lived test server under a stale execution worktree; detection must come from DDx ownership, cwd/worktree, and liveness evidence, not broad command-name matching.
4. `TestExecutionCleanupManager_UnsupportedPlatformReportsProcessCleanupUnavailable` (or a build-tagged equivalent) proves unsupported platforms take a deterministic no-op/report-unavailable path.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Cleanup.*Process|AttemptDescendant|OrphanHarness|Executor|DockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup' -count=1` passes.
6. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -count=1` passes and `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:iterate, area:agent, area:cleanup, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, operator-ux, resource-exhaustion, incident:niflheim-2026-06-08, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-2f8e5f26" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="199d1d6d319b47364ff51011121491c6c979d4e7" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260628T023501-16ae1b4b"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

`ddx cleanup --apply` can delete stale DDx execution files, but it cannot terminate stale DDx-owned attempt-descendant process groups that are provably orphaned. Heavy leftover processes can continue burning CPU or holding ports after their original attempt is gone.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:230-527` applies file cleanup for stale temp dirs, worktrees, scratch roots, stale run-state files, evidence dirs, logs, worker dirs, and refs, but contains no safe process-group termination path.
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:110-138` has no process reaping counts or warnings in `ExecutionCleanupSummary`, so apply mode cannot report terminated, preserved, blocked, or unsafe process groups.
- `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:82-100` and `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper.go:155-168` intentionally gate startup harness reaping on top-level harness names and dead bead lease owners; that does not cover descendant process groups whose harness root has already exited.
- `cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:10-29` sets process group and parent-death attributes for normal harness children, which cleanup can use only when stale ownership and liveness evidence prove the group is DDx-owned and safe to reap.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend execution cleanup apply mode with an injectable process-group killer and conservative stale ownership checks. Apply may terminate only process groups associated with the current project's DDx execution root where run-state cleanup liveness is expired or missing, the recorded owner PID is gone, the worktree is under the current project execution root, and no live worker sidecar or active registered worktree owns it. Preserve live run-state, live worker sidecar, active registered worktree, other-project processes, and any uncertain candidate. Prefer graceful terminate followed by bounded kill where feasible. Surface blocked warnings instead of silently deleting files when a process is detected but cannot be killed safely.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go` and adjacent new platform-specific process cleanup files/tests.
- `cli/internal/workerstatus/*` or `cli/internal/agent/orphan_harness_reaper*` only for reused process-scanning helpers, not broad rewrites.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md` and `docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md` only if this child changes the documented cleanup contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not edit Niflheim.
